summary:
To protect the dental pulp from infection while preserving as much natural tooth structure as possible, an incomplete debridement allows part of the decayed area to stay undisturbed during treatment of deep cavities. This approach helps maintain structural integrity and promotes better long-term outcomes by minimizing invasive procedures. \boxed{} Agent2 concludes that preventing pulp exposure and maintaining a small amount of viable dentin are crucial reasons behind using this technique when dealing with extensive caries. 

The summary captures two main aspects highlighted earlier—protecting the pulp against damage or inflammation due to over-debriding, and retaining enough sound enamel/dentin substrate necessary for proper restoration without compromising future health risks associated too aggressive treatments. Thus,
\[ \textbf{\box{(the rationale provided matches most closely)} }.\]
